architecture.summary: ArmoRM is a two-stage reward modeling system for RLHF. Stage 1: an Absolute-Rating Multi-Objective Reward Model (ArmoRM) trained on multi-dimensional absolute-rating preference data, where each dimension corresponds to a human-interpretable objective (e.g., honesty, verbosity, safety). Stage 2: a Mixture-of-Experts strategy with a gating network (shallow MLP on top of ArmoRM) that automatically selects the most suitable reward objectives based on context. The system produces interpretable reward scores by decomposing preference into human-readable dimensions and dynamically weighting them.

biblio.title: Interpretable Preferences via Multi-Objective Reward Modeling and Mixture-of-Experts

claims.statement: ArmoRM-Llama3-8B obtains state-of-the-art performance on RewardBench, a benchmark evaluating RMs for language modeling.
The performance of ArmoRM-Llama3-8B surpasses the LLM-as-a-judge method with GPT-4 judges by a margin.
ArmoRM-Llama3-8B approaches the performance of the much larger Nemotron-4 340B reward model.
Conventional reward models lack interpretability; their outputs cannot be intuitively understood by humans regarding why an RM thinks a response is good or not.
ArmoRM trains on multi-dimensional absolute-rating data where each dimension corresponds to a human-interpretable objective (e.g., honesty, verbosity, safety), enabling interpretable preferences.
